Plaintiff Norman Herterichs Notice Of Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NORMAN HERTERICH'S Motion For New Trial is denied. None of the grounds asserted by plaintiff supports disturbing the grant of summary judgment in favor of defendant Alice Traeg. The undisputed facts leave establish that plaintiff could not reasonably rely on the probate petition in prosecuting an omitted child proceeding. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice of exhibits A and B is granted. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL L. BOLI, ESQ. of Law Office of Michael L. Boli, Tel No. 415-362-9001 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ANDREW A. KAPUR, ESQ., Tel No. 415-279-4264 appearing for Defendant Alice Traeg.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALICE TRAEG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff fails to show that defendant committed a tort because plaintiff cannot demonstrate reasonable reliance as a matter of law. Plaintiff claims that he was misled into prosecuting an omitted child proceeding because the probate petition falsely stated that decedent had no children. The undisputed evidence establishes that decedent's will was attached to that petition and the will expressly stated that decedent disinherits all heirs that are not mentioned in the will. Plaintiff's decision to pursue the omitted child procedure was unreasonable as a matter of law because: 1) the decedent was aware of plaintiff based upon decedent's numerous child support payments and 2) the explicit disinheritance clause. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL L. BOLI, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-9001 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. THOMAS C. TAGLIARINI, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-0692 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ARNDT PELTNER;
ATTY. ANDREW A. KAPUR, TEL. NO. 1-415-279-4264 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ALECE TRAEG.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ARNDT PELTNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And For Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted because Plaintiff cannot establish that he has suffered damages, which is a required element for each of Plaintiff's causes of action. (Tagliarini Decl. Exhibit 9.) A moving party is entitled to summary judgment as a matter of law if the party can establish that there is no genuine issue of material fact. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 845.) The tort of another doctrine does not apply to the case at hand. In order to invoke the doctrine, Plaintiff must show that because of Defendant's tortious conduct, Plaintiff was forced to litigate against a third party. Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendant committed a tort because he cannot demonstrate that he suffered any damage as a result of Defendant's conduct. The Court of Appeal's finding, in ruling on Defendant's motion for summary judgment in the underlying probate case, that Plaintiff had no interest in decedent's estate establishes that Plaintiff has not suffered any damage as a result of Defendant's alleged tortious conduct. (Tagliarini Decl. Exhibit 9.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
